headline: Ukrainian drone maker UDD Tech selects Holland (Lucas County) for first U.S. manufacturing center — 300 jobs, $18.4M, Pentagon Drone Dominance contract
paragraphs: UDD Tech Corporation — the U.S. entity representing Ukraine's F-Drones, which has more than three years of battlefield-tested drone development experience — has selected the Village of Holland in Lucas County, Ohio for its first U.S. assembly and manufacturing center. The announcement was part of Governor Mike DeWine's June 29 package of seven Ohio projects expected to create 1,221 jobs and more than $1.1 billion in total investment. UDD Tech will invest $18.4 million and create at least 300 jobs by December 31, 2029. The Ohio Tax Credit Authority approved a 1.642%, nine-year Job Creation Tax Credit for the project. Congresswoman Marcy Kaptur, JobsOhio (President and CEO JP Nauseef), and the Regional Growth Partnership were partners in securing the commitment.

The Holland facility will produce first-person-view (FPV) drones and unmanned systems for the U.S. Department of Defense's Drone Dominance Program — a $1 billion initiative targeting the purchase of more than 200,000 low-cost, consumable drones by 2027. UDD ranked sixth overall in Phase 1 of the program's competitive 'Gauntlet' evaluation and has already secured a U.S. government prototype contract for the delivery of 2,000 FPV drones. The facility will also support allied-nation demand, training and testing operations, and dual-use commercial drone applications. F-Drones CEO Stas Khutor has positioned the Lucas County site as UDD's production platform for scaling U.S.-based drone manufacturing under the DoD's drive to build a domestic industrial base capable of countering low-cost adversarial drone threats at scale.
why_it_matters: UDD Tech's selection of Lucas County is the first defense-technology manufacturing investment in the Toledo metro tied directly to a Pentagon procurement program — a category distinct from the advanced automotive, glass, and industrial manufacturing that has historically defined northwest Ohio's economic identity. The Drone Dominance Program creates a durable federal demand signal for FPV drone production at scale; UDD's early ranking in the Gauntlet competition positions the Holland facility to grow with that program's output targets. For RGP, JobsOhio NW Ohio, and the City of Toledo's economic development pitch, this win adds a defense-tech dimension to the region's site-selector story — alongside the QTS Van Wert data center campus — signaling that northwest Ohio is competing for and capturing investment categories that did not exist in the region's portfolio three years ago.
